The Program

We built what didnʼt exist.

Most accelerators are built around one goal: exit. Sell fast, scale fast, cash out. FoundHer was built around a different question: what if wāhine in Hawaiʻi could build businesses that let them stay?

Since 2021, weʼve backed 24 companies, delivered over $480K in non-dilutive investment, and watched founders triple their revenue and build wholesale accounts, childcare stability, and community. Weʼre not slowing down.

Our Mission

To build economic sovereignty and create lasting community impact through wāhine leadership.

Our Vision

A transformed economic landscape, rooted in cooperation, cultural grounding, and regenerative practice where businesses uplift community and ʻāina.

Women-led businesses are less risky, deliver greater ROI, and multiply their impact across community. Yet the funding gap persists. FoundHer closes it with capital, care, and a curriculum built through an indigenous cultural lens.

What You Get

◆ {{ b }}

Curriculum

Real business skills taught through an indigenous cultural lens.

{{ c.d }}

Is FoundHer For You?

Find your program.

Pae ʻĀina

Youʼre based anywhere in Hawaiʻi. Your business can be in any industry.

Kapalili

Youʼre based on Hawaiʻi Island. Your business is in agriculture, food production, or food systems.

To join either program youʼll need

✓{{ e }}

How We Select Our Cohort

We look for founders ready to grow, and who lead with values.

From Our ʻOhana

Revenue growth
6
Jobs created
1
Flagship storefront

“Our quality of life at home has improved significantly, we travel, we've purchased real estate, we're planning for ours kids' future. FoundHer didn't just help me grow my business, it helped me understand my business.”

Taleea Carvalho · Wailua Granola Company · Kauaʻi · Cohort 3

3×
Revenue in Year 1
76%
Profit margin
2×
Pays farmers market rate

“None of this would be possible without FoundHer. There is nothing like this. We are positioned for longevity rather than just pure profit. We want to be sustainable because we actually care about what we are doing.”

Malie Sarsona · Waimea Herb Company · Hawaiʻi Island · Cohort 1

Our Mentors

Our mentors are the heartbeat of this program. We are deeply grateful to each of them for showing up, sharing their wisdom, and investing their time in the next generation of wāhine founders. Each company in our cohort is paired with two dedicated mentors throughout the 6 months.

Our Story

Five years. Six cohorts. And our moʻolelo still in kākau mode.

Operating Partner

The Purple Maiʻa Foundation is on a mission to educate and empower the next generation of culturally grounded, community-serving technology makers and problem solvers.
